zone_summary: CORS policy now exposes the x-ms-continuation-token response header so browser JavaScript can read pagination tokens from cross-origin responses.

behavioral_changes.summary: The service CORS policy now emits exposure metadata for the x-ms-continuation-token header, allowing browser-based clients to access that pagination header from cross-origin responses.

grounding_evidence.reason: Adds x-ms-continuation-token to the ASP.NET Core CORS exposed-header allowlist, which drives the Access-Control-Expose-Headers response contract for browser callers.

scenario_sketches.title: Cross-origin responses expose x-ms-continuation-token through the CORS policy
scenario_sketches.rationale: Verify that a request matching the configured CORS policy produces an Access-Control-Expose-Headers value containing x-ms-continuation-token, establishing that browser JavaScript can read the pagination continuation header.
